```json
  "findings": [
    "Copper tube ends were cut but not reamed, leaving a visible internal burr.",
    "Internal burr creates a localized flow restriction and turbulence point.",
    "Burr can accelerate erosion/corrosion and damage downstream valves, fittings, or tubing.",
    "Condition violates standard copper joining practice and manufacturer reaming requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove or isolate the affected copper section.",
    "Ream all cut copper tube ends to remove internal burr and create a smooth, chamfered edge.",
    "Inspect for debris, clean joints, and reassemble or resolder as required.",
    "Re-test or inspect the system before closing out."
  ],
  "rationale": "Copper tubing must be reamed after cutting to remove the internal burr. A visible burr is out of tolerance because it restricts flow, creates turbulence, and can damage components downstream. This is a non-compliant rough-in condition that must be corrected before the work proceeds to inspection or finish.",
  "references": [
    "IPC (International Plumbing Code) - general plumbing installation standards",
    "Copper tube manufacturer specifications and joining instructions requiring reaming and deburring"
  ]
}
```
